row.problem_statement:
Change default code style to `ktlint_official`
Starting of ktlint `1.0` the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The  `ktlint_official` codestyle  adheres to the best parts of  the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android Kotlint styleguid](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). But it no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which  lacks from bug which still are not resolved after multiple years.

row.patch:
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
index 2770232846..6c8f94e2e5 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -21,6 +21,7 @@ This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 * Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 * Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
 * Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
+* Fix false positive regarding property that potentially can contain data which is mutable. `property-naming` [#2140](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2140)
 * Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
 * Fix null pointer exception for if-else statement with empty THEN block `if-else-bracing` [#2135](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2135)
 
diff --git a/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md b/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md
index 5e57426856..0c64d7cc75 100644
--- a/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md
+++ b/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md
@@ -637,20 +637,26 @@ Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 
 Enforce naming of property.
 
+!!! note
+    This rule can not reliably detect all situations in which incorrect property naming is used. So it only detects in which it is certain that naming is incorrect.
+
 === "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 
     ```kotlin
-    val FOO_1 = Foo()
-    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
+    val foo1 = Foo() // In case developer want to communicate that Foo is mutable
+    val FOO1 = Foo() // In case developer want to communicate that Foo is deeply immutable
 
-    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
+    const val FOO_BAR = "FOO-BAR" // By definition deeply immutable
+
+    var foo2: Foo = Foo() // By definition not immutable
 
     class Bar {
-        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
-        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
+        val foo1 = Foo() // In case developer want to communicate that Foo is mutable
+        val FOO1 = Foo() // In case developer want to communicate that Foo is deeply immutable
+
+        const val FOO_BAR = "FOO-BAR" // By definition deeply immutable
 
-        val foo2 = "foo"
-        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
+        var foo2: Foo = Foo() // By definition not immutable
 
         // Backing property
         private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
@@ -661,17 +667,12 @@ Enforce naming of property.
 === "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 
     ```kotlin
-    val Foo1 = Foo()
-    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
+    const val fooBar = "FOO-BAR" // By definition deeply immutable
 
-    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
+    var FOO2: Foo = Foo() // By definition not immutable
 
     class Bar {
-        const val foo2 = "foo"
-        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
-
-        val FOO2 = "foo"
-        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
+        val FOO_BAR = "FOO-BAR" // Class properties always start with lowercase, const is not allowed
 
         // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
         private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
@@ -683,9 +684,6 @@ Enforce naming of property.
     }
     ```
 
-!!! note
-    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
-
 This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
 
 Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
diff --git a/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt b/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt
index 79f1e477a7..0e5d2f7120 100644
--- a/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt
+++ b/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt
@@ -45,17 +45,15 @@ public class PropertyNamingRule :
             .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
             ?.let { identifier ->
                 when {
-                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
+                    property.hasConstModifier() -> {
+                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
+                    }
+                    property.hasCustomGetter() || property.isTopLevelValue() || property.isObjectValue() -> {
                         // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
                     }
                     property.isBackingProperty() -> {
                         visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
                     }
-                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
-                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
-                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
-                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
-                    }
                     else -> {
                         visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
                     }

row.problem_statement:
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE - https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#non-constant_names
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val SOME_MAP: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Create object, with mutable collection property. 

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.50.0

In my case it is an object's property. I did not check for other cases as top level or companion object's variables (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming should not be applied to class properties according to https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names).

+    private fun visitTryCatchFinally(node: ASTNode) {
+        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
+        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
+        node
+            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
+            ?.let { finally ->
+                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
+                    fromAstNode = finally,
+                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
+                    childIndent = "",
+                ).prevCodeLeaf()
+            }
+
+        node
+            .findChildByType(CATCH)
+            ?.let { catch ->
+                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
+                    fromAstNode = catch,
+                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
+                    childIndent = "",
+                ).prevCodeLeaf()
+            }
+
+        startIndentContext(
+            fromAstNode = node,
+            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
+            lastChildIndent = "",
+        )
+    }

row.problem_statement:
False positive on indent with try/catch and typealias
## Expected Behavior
The following code is valid:

```
fun test() = try {
  1
}
catch(_: Throwable) {
  2
}
```

and

```
typealias Foo =
  HashMap<Bar, Baz?>
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint formats the code like this:

```
fun test() = try {
  1
}
  catch(_: Throwable) {
    2
  }
```

and

```
typealias Foo =
HashMap<Bar, Baz?>
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run `ktlint --format`

.editorconfig:

```
[*.{kt,kts}]
ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false
ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
indent_size = 2
indent_style = space
insert_final_newline = true
ktlint_experimental = enabled
ktlint_experimental_comment-wrapping = disabled
ktlint_experimental_function-naming = disabled
ktlint_experimental_function-signature = disabled
ktlint_experimental_property-naming = disabled
ktlint_standard_annotation = disabled
ktlint_standard_filename = disabled
ktlint_standard_keyword-spacing = disabled
ktlint_standard_max-line-length = disabled
ktlint_standard_no-semi = disabled
ktlint_standard_package-name = disabled
ktlint_standard_spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations = disabled
ktlint_standard_string-template = disabled
max_line_length = 120
```

The formatting is correct when ktlint 0.47.1 is used

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.2
* Operating System and version: Ubuntu 22.04

Accidental double-KDoc reported as consecutive line space problem
## Expected Behavior

I have no idea. Maybe there should be a rule for this? Useless KDoc rule?


## Observed Behavior

Consecutive blank line rule triggers despite there not being consecutive blank lines.

## Steps to Reproduce

```kotlin
  /**
   * True to throw a [JsonDataException] on any attempt to call [skipValue].
   *
   * ## Getting
   * Returns true if this parser forbids skipping names and values.
   *
   * ## Setting
   * Configure whether this parser throws a [JsonDataException] when [skipValue] is
   * called. By default this parser permits values to be skipped.
   *
   * Forbid skipping to prevent unrecognized values from being silently ignored. This option is
   * useful in development and debugging because it means a typo like "locatiom" will be detected
   * early. It's potentially harmful in production because it complicates revising a JSON schema.
   */
  /** Returns true if this parser forbids skipping names and values. */
  @get:JvmName("failOnUnknown")
  public var failOnUnknown: Boolean = false
```

Error on line: 219, column: 1
rule: no-consecutive-blank-lines

```

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.2
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Spotless `spotApply` task

    */
Tnx, this is reproducable.

> Maybe there should be a rule for this? Useless KDoc rule?

Ktlint `0.49.0` will get a new rule `no-consecutive-comments` (#1454) which also detects consecutive KDoc's.

row.problem_statement:
Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_
row.hints_text:
The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

--- a/CHANGELOG.md

 * When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
 * Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
 * A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))
+* Wrap annotations on type arguments in same way as with other constructs `annotation`, `wrapping` ([#1725](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1725))
 
 ### Changed
 * Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).

row.problem_statement:
A type with multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList is not formatted correctly
Code below:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>,
)
```

is formatted as:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

KtLint: 0.47.1

_Originally posted by @Vadgyik in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/673#issuecomment-1351480443_

+
+    @Test
+    fun `Given a rangeUntil operator`() {
+        val code =
+            """
+            @OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
+            fun foo(int: Int): String =
+                when (int) {

+                    in 20..< 30 -> "B"

+                    in 40 ..<50 -> "D"
+                    else -> "unknown"

+            """.trimIndent()
+        val formattedCode =
+            """
+            @OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
+            fun foo(int: Int): String =
+                when (int) {
+                    in 10..<20 -> "A"
+                    in 20..<30 -> "B"
+                    in 30..<40 -> "C"
+                    in 40..<50 -> "D"
+                    else -> "unknown"
+                }
+            """.trimIndent()
+        spacingAroundRangeOperatorRuleAssertThat(code)
+            .hasLintViolations(

+                LintViolation(6, 15, "Unexpected spacing around \"..<\""),

+            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
+    }
 }
row.problem_statement:
Spacing around `..<` should be identical to spacing around `..`
Given code below with inconsistent spacing around `..<`:
```
@OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
fun foo(int: Int): String =
    when (int) {
        in 10..<20 -> "A"
        in 20..< 30 -> "B"
        in 30 ..< 40 -> "C"
        in 40 ..<50 -> "D"
        else -> "unknown"
    }
```

should be formatted as:
```
@OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
fun foo(int: Int): String =
    when (int) {
        in 10..<20 -> "A"
        in 20..<30 -> "B"
        in 30..<40 -> "C"
        in 40..<50 -> "D"
        else -> "unknown"
    }
```

Unexpected indentation level after newline with 0.50.0
With version 0.50.0, the below code now produces an error because of the lack of newline after the {:
```
{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```
What seems odd to me is that the "correct" formatting looks like the below:
```
{
        size ->
    icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await()
}
```
This plays out the same for larger code blocks as well where the first line after the bracket is indented further over than all subsequent ones if there's a separator involved. It seems to me that if anything, we'd want to invert that indentation to something more like:
```
{
    size ->
        icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await()
}
```
row.hints_text:
This problem has the same root cause as #2106 and will be fixed via that issue.

The double indentation of parameters inside the lambda looks indeed weird at first sight. It is based on (but not identical to) the default formatting of IntelliJ IDEA.

Given the functional literal below (note that it contains multiple parameters on separate lines):
```
val foo = { a,
        b,
        c ->
    doSomething()
}
```
It is formatted by IntelliJ IDEA formatter as:
```
val foo = { a,
            b,
            c ->
    doSomething()
}
```
Analog to formatting of functions having parameters on separate lines, ktlint formats this as:
```
val foo = {
        a,
        b,
        c ->
    doSomething()
}
```

The format above should only be applied when having a function literal with parameters on separate lines. As result of the bug in #2101, your parameter list was wrapped and as of that changed into a multiline parameter list. As a result of that, the indentation of a multiline parameter list was applied. With fix of #2106 you original code sample will be kept unchanged.

row.problem_statement:
`wrapping`: raw string false positive `Missing newline after "{"`
I have some code using `let` blocks inside raw strings, something like:
```
package test

class QueryGenerator {
    fun getQueryString(query: QueryRequest): String {
        val q = """
            SELECT *
            FROM table
            WHERE 1 = 1
            ${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}    
        """
        return q
    }
}

data class QueryRequest(
    val gameId: String?,
)
```

## Expected Behavior
This should cause no violations of the `wrapping` rule, regardless of the string size.

## Observed Behavior
In the code above, if I replace `table` by `table_name_long_enough_so_max_line_length_is_met_and_wrapping_rule_is_violated_and_generates_an_error`, for example (anything that makes the raw string size go above `max_line_length` seems to work), then I start seeing:
```
QueryGenerator.kt:9:34: Missing newline after "{" (wrapping)
```

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start with the code above
2. Make the raw string big enough
3. Run ktlint 0.48+ in the file

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: `max_line_length = 160`
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint CLI
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux (rolling as of 2023-01-15)
row.hints_text:
This issue is very tricky to reproduce, actually, this might not be related to `max_line_length` at all. I just tried moving the entire content of the raw string to a single line and the error is gone, even with a very long raw string.
The exact values I provided causes the issue 100% of the times I tried, though, so I hope that's enough to reproduce the issue on your side.
Can be reproduced. The error is introduced in `0.48.x`.
Thanks for the quick reply :)
This is my only blocker to update to 0.48, so let me know if I can help with anything.
You can try to solve this issue via a PR ;-)

Otherwise. just disable the rule (https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#how-do-i-suppress-errors-for-a-lineblockfile) and start using the new version.

Formatting enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas causes rule violations on first run
## Expected Behavior
<!---Tell us what should happen. -->
<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->

When formatting and validating the following enum class, the ktlint command should exit successfully (status `0`):

```kotlin
enum class TestEnum() {
    A,
    B;

    companion object {
        const val A_NUMBER = 1L
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
<!---Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- Provide the exact command which was executed but please -->
<!--- ensure that the flag "--log-level=debug" is added to the -->
<!--- command as well. Provide the output of this command. -->

When running ktlint on the above enum class, it is formatted correctly, but the run fails with rule violations (exit status `1`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format 
17:25:00.694 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:1:1: Missing trailing comma before "}" (trailing-comma-on-declaration-site)
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:5:4: Unnecessary semicolon (no-semi)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  no-semi: 1
  trailing-comma-on-declaration-site: 1
Exit status: 1 
```

I note that the line number for the `no-semi` violation is the line number after formatting.

A second run on the (now properly formatted) code runs correctly (exit status `0`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format
17:25:05.397 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
Exit status: 0
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run command on above enum class:
```ktlint --format```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux
row.hints_text:
I have the same issue

See release notes of 0.48.0:
> * The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))

@paul-dingemans I don't quite follow. My issue is that after doing formatting, ktlint reports invalid  `no-semi` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` rule violations. A subsequent run of ktlint (correctly) passes the same code.

Ah now I see. Although it is not visual in the textual representation of the code, the trailing comma is not inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code. I will try to fix it.

row.problem_statement:
Do not merge annotated expression with function signature rule
Given code below:
```
private fun getErrorMessage(value: String): String =
        @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
        customMessage.takeUnless { it.isEmpty() } ?: String.format(DEFAULT_ERROR_MESSAGE_WITH_NO_REASON, value)
```
*and* assume that the `function-signature` rule is enabled *and* the (first line of the) function body expression has to be merged with the function signature.

For example with `.editorconfig`:
```
[{*.kt,*.kts}]
ktlint_code_style = android
ktlint_standard=enabled
ktlint_experimental=enabled
```
the command `ktlint --relative` results in
```
17:41:39.417 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
x.kts:6:97: Unnecessary trailing comma before ")" (standard:trailing-comma-on-call-site)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:1:53: First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature (standard:function-signature)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:1: Exceeded max line length (100) (standard:max-line-length)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:31: Missing newline after "{" (standard:wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:46: Expected newline (standard:annotation)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:64: Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line) (standard:argument-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:102: Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line) (standard:argument-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:107: Missing newline before ")" (standard:argument-list-wrapping)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  standard:argument-list-wrapping: 3
  standard:annotation: 1
  standard:function-signature: 1
  standard:max-line-length: 1
  standard:trailing-comma-on-call-site: 1
  standard:wrapping: 1
```
When running `format` the output changes to:
```
17:44:18.369 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:1:53: Expected newline before annotation (standard:annotation)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  standard:annotation: 1
```
This log message is rather confusing when you compare it with the original code sample. However, it should not be compared with the original but with the modified code to make sense.
and the file is formatted as:
```
private fun getErrorMessage(value: String): String = @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
customMessage.takeUnless { it.isEmpty() }
    ?: String.format(DEFAULT_ERROR_MESSAGE_WITH_NO_REASON, value)
```

The `function-signature` should not format in such a way that if violates the `annotation` rule.

_Originally posted by @paul-dingemans in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2028#issuecomment-1551678889_

+package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental.internal
+
+/**
+ * Transforms a string containing regular expression ranges like "A-Z" and "a-z" to a RegEx which checks whether a
+ * unicode character has an uppercase versus a lowercase mapping to a letter. This function intents to keep the original
+ * expression more readable
+ */
+internal fun String.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters() =

+        .replace("a-z", "\\p{Ll}")
+        .toRegex()

row.problem_statement:
0.48.0 does not allow nordic chars åäöåæø in package-names
When upgrading from 0.47.1 to 0.48.0, ktlint complains on package names containing åæø.

## Expected Behavior
Expecting æøå to be accepted

## Observed Behavior
` Package name contains a disallowed character (cannot be auto-corrected) (package-name)`

## Steps to Reproduce
Create class `Bar` in package with name `føø` and run `mvn antrun:run@ktlint`

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Apache Maven 3.8.1
* Operating System and version: macOS Ventura 13.1
row.hints_text:
Similar to https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1530
More problems like this are likely to occur in this release. `0.48.0` contains some rules which formerly where experimental but now have been added to the `standard` rules. Also new `experimental` naming rules have been added in `0.48.x`. Can you please run ktlint with `--experimental` once to identify which of the newly added experimental rules also break on nordic characters?
It seems like it also fails on property-, class- and function-names containing nordic characters when running with experimental rules:

`Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:property-naming)" on properties containing nordic characters.`
`Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:class-naming)`
`Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:function-naming)`
Indeed, that is not entirely unexpected. Thnx for confirming though. Sorry for the inconvenience.

row.problem_statement: Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur
row.hints_text:
Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem.

The code below is comparable to your example:
```
// Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right                  -> |
val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
```

The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in:
```
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping)
```
From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code.

Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below:
```
// Assume, next line exceeds the max line length                            -> |
val foo1: (
        (
            s1: String,
            s2: String,
            s3: String,
            s4: String,
            s5: String,
            s6: String
        ) -> Unit
    )? = null
```
but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well:
```
src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping)
```

So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following:
```
// Assume, next line exceeds the max line length                            -> |
val foo1: (
    (
    s1: String,
    s2: String,
    s3: String,
    s4: String,
    s5: String,
    s6: String
) -> Unit
)? = null
```
This is horrible code.

For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line:
```
val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping
```

Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such.

diff --git a/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarterMutexTest.kt b/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarterMutexTest.kt
new file mode 100644
index 000000000000..611e018567a2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarterMutexTest.kt
@@ -0,0 +1,142 @@
+package org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads
+
+import android.content.Context
+import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData
+import kotlinx.coroutines.ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
+import kotlinx.coroutines.asCoroutineDispatcher
+import kotlinx.coroutines.delay
+import org.junit.Before
+import org.junit.Test
+import org.mockito.Mock
+import org.mockito.kotlin.any
+import org.mockito.kotlin.doAnswer
+import org.mockito.kotlin.doSuspendableAnswer
+import org.mockito.kotlin.mock
+import org.mockito.kotlin.whenever
+import org.wordpress.android.BaseUnitTest
+import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
+import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.PostModel
+import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PageStore
+import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostStore
+import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
+import org.wordpress.android.util.NetworkUtilsWrapper
+import org.wordpress.android.util.analytics.AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
+import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.helpers.ConnectionStatus
+import java.util.concurrent.Executors
+
+/**
+ * This class tries to reproduce the situation of trying to start the upload service when the device is in the
+ * background. Such situation causes the system to throw a IllegalStateException, as it's not allowed to create
+ * services from the background, which in turns causes a failure in the coroutine jobs, which should be cancelled
+ * without crashing the application.
+ *
+ * This was created because the situation above was causing a crash in our app:
+ * https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?project=573168
+ */
+@OptIn(ExperimentalCoroutinesApi::class)
+class UploadStarterMutexTest : BaseUnitTest() {
+    private val bgDispatcher = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(DEFAULT_THREADS).asCoroutineDispatcher()
+    private val ioDispatcher = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(IO_THREADS).asCoroutineDispatcher()
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var context: Context
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var dispatcher: Dispatcher
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var postStore: PostStore
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var pageStore: PageStore
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var siteStore: SiteStore
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var uploadActionUseCase: UploadActionUseCase
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var tracker: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var uploadServiceFacade: UploadServiceFacade
+
+    @Mock
+    lateinit var networkUtilsWrapper: NetworkUtilsWrapper
+
+    private var connectionStatus: MutableLiveData<ConnectionStatus> = MutableLiveData()
+
+    private lateinit var uploadStarter: UploadStarter
+
+    @Before
+    fun setUp() {
+        uploadStarter = UploadStarter(
+            context = context,
+            dispatcher = dispatcher,
+            postStore = postStore,
+            pageStore = pageStore,
+            siteStore = siteStore,
+            uploadActionUseCase = uploadActionUseCase,
+            tracker = tracker,
+            bgDispatcher = bgDispatcher,
+            ioDispatcher = ioDispatcher,
+            uploadServiceFacade = uploadServiceFacade,
+            networkUtilsWrapper = networkUtilsWrapper,
+            connectionStatus = connectionStatus
+        )
+    }
+
+    @Test
+    fun `should not crash if uploadPost throws an exception`() = test {
+        // network is available
+        whenever(networkUtilsWrapper.isNetworkAvailable()).thenReturn(true)
+        // return 10 sites for queuing
+        whenever(siteStore.sites).thenReturn(List(10) { mock() })
+        // each site has 10 posts with local changes
+        whenever(postStore.getPostsWithLocalChanges(any())).doAnswer {
+            Thread.sleep(10)
+            List(10) { fakePostModel(it, false) }
+        }
+        // each site has 5 pages with local changes
+        whenever(pageStore.getPagesWithLocalChanges(any())).doSuspendableAnswer {
+            delay(10)
+            List(5) {
+                fakePostModel(
+                    100 + it,
+                    true
+                )
+            }
+        }
+
+        whenever(uploadActionUseCase.getAutoUploadAction(any(), any()))
+            .thenReturn(UploadActionUseCase.UploadAction.UPLOAD)
+
+        // throw IllegalStateException when uploading post (emulate trying to start background service)
+        whenever(uploadServiceFacade.uploadPost(any(), any<PostModel>(), any()))
+            .thenThrow(IllegalStateException("FAKE: Not allowed to start service intent"))
+
+        // ACT
+        uploadStarter.queueUploadFromAllSites().join()
+    }
+
+    companion object {
+        // # of threads in Dispatchers.Default is equal to the # of CPU cores, 8 is a common # of cores in modern phones
+        // https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines/-dispatchers/-default.html
+        private const val DEFAULT_THREADS = 8
+
+        // default # of threads in Dispatchers.IO is 64 but it can be even larger, let's use 64, which is enough
+        // https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines/-dispatchers/-i-o.html
+        private const val IO_THREADS = 64
+
+        private fun fakePostModel(
+            id: Int,
+            isPage: Boolean,
+        ) = PostModel().apply {
+            setId(id)
+            setIsPage(isPage)
+            setTitle("Title $id")
+            setStatus("draft")
+        }
+    }
+}

This is not a duplicate of #17463 since this is now happening on coroutines 1.7.1 which fixed the underlying root cause of that other ticket. This has a similar stacktrace, but it looks like we might be misusing the Mutex in our `UploadStarter` implementation.
row.hints_text:
**TLDR:** I think our code is silently making the crash look like it’s an internal Mutex problem but in reality, we might be calling `unlock` unnecessarily when the Mutex internals are not expecting it. We should remove [this line](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186) and I think it might solve our issues on coroutines 1.7.1.

Based on the [breadcrumbs](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?project=5731682&referrer=issue-stream&sort=freq&statsPeriod=14d&stream_index=1#breadcrumbs) I believe the actual issue is that we tried to [start the post upload service](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L175-L179) while the app was in the background and the system didn’t let us, which caused all `upload` jobs (created [here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L138-L142)) to be canceled since the [Parent Job](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L68) is not a `SupervisorJob`.

This made all jobs (except the first one which failed because of an `IllegalStateException`) [fall here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L183-L187) (as can be seen in the breadcrumb logs).

**What I first thought could be happening is:**

We use the `bgDispatcher` ([Dispatchers.Default](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/e03d208247c7487a7497f873d91f6b0e52a3622e/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/modules/ThreadModule.kt#L40-L42)) which has several threads in the pool and we use the same `mutex` for all upload jobs. Because of that, multiple coroutines (running in different threads) are suspended awaiting the to [acquire](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L196-L201C6) the semaphore (which [creates a queue of segments](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L285-L315)).

Since multiple jobs are being canceled at once, I think that the cancellation was not able to be properly propagated and the `segment` state for one of them was not marked as `CANCELLED` making the `Semaphore#tryResumeNextFromQueue` ([which is called on](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/1.7.1/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L265) `Semaphore#release` [via the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L216) `Mutex#unlock`) [call the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L329-L341) `tryResumeAcquire`. Which in turn [calls](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L347C23-L347C23) `Mutex#tryResume` but since that is canceled it runs the `onCancellation` [callback](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255) when trying to resume the underlying continuation, which is [where the crash is happening](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L250-L262).

But I am not 100% sure about that, as I feel like this scenario would be pretty common for a synchronization mechanism using coroutines (like `Mutex` and `Semaphore`), which is why I believe that `onCancellation` callback also unlocks the `Mutex`, in case of that late propagation of the cancellation to the mutex `Segment`.

**So, considering that the code makes sense and works (hopefully) what I think might be the issue is:**

We also have a [call to](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186) `unlock` on our side and from the breadcrumbs we know that the code is reaching that line. So it might be that this `unlock` is actually unlocking the mutex **at the same time as** the whole cancellation flow I mentioned above is happening (_which would be possible because of the multiple threads in the Dispatcher pool_), so when the cancelation reaches the `onCancellation` [callback here](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255) our code already unlocked it, causing the crash to happen in the `Mutex` internals.

This is my best guess, but the Mutex/Semaphore code is a bit complex and I didn’t fully understand it yet (and probably never will 😆).

### Suggestion
Remove that `unlock` [call ASAP](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186) as we really don’t need it since we use `withLock` which is better in 2 ways:
1. It makes sure to `unlock` when exceptions happen ([try/finally](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L120-L131))
2. It only calls `unlock` if it was successfully locked before running the `action`, so if my hypothesis above is correct, the internal `lockSuspend` code will cause a cancellation before reaching that `try/finally` block

row.problem_statement:
🤖 Cancel stale requests on domain search screen
When searching for a new domain (to purchase) within the domain management flow, the results of multiple requests are loaded sequentially after typing. Only the latest request should be used to update the pending / loading screen.

Note that this is a separate concern from the debounce delay (used to throttle requests and reduce wasted fetches on keystrokes). Increasing the debounce delay _could_ mitigate the buggy results ux, but this has a tradeoff of increasing the total time to success. Instead, we should ensure that only the response to the latest request can update the ui state.

Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-1RW](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/3857462344/?referrer=github_integration)
Checking the sentry logs all of them contain the following error:
```
e/WordPress-SITE_CREATION: Site already exists - seems like an issue with domain suggestions endpoint

StackTrace: com.android.volley.ClientError
	at com.android.volley.toolbox.NetworkUtility.shouldRetryException(NetworkUtility.java:193)

[Checking the Volley code](https://github.com/google/volley/blob/master/core/src/main/java/com/android/volley/toolbox/NetworkUtility.java#L191) the above should indicate that no retry is possible.

row.problem_statement:
[Reader IA] Bring back the Discover Onboarding for new users
When a new user entered the Reader for the first time we would show the `Discover` feed with a "Welcome" message and a button leading to a "Choose your topics" screen, which essentially followed those tags and made Discover be populated with stuff based on those tags.

All the code for that is still present in the app and is still shown if the user unsubscribes from ALL their tags (when the user has 0 subscribed tags), here's an example:

https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/7b77b04a-fa3d-4e6a-852c-95ac587b97cd

This (0 tags subscribed) used to be the state of a new account as well, but recently the `dailyprompt` tag is being subscribed to by default on new accounts, which causes the Welcome screen to be skipped and the Reader Discover feed to be populated, which makes it hard for the user to configure their tags and see interesting stuff in that feed for the first time accessing the Reader in the app.

We want to bring back this simple Onboarding Flow to provide a better user experience, especially on the first time using the Reader.

row.problem_statement:
[Reader IA] Show lists directly on dropdown menu if there are 2 or fewer
We are currently grouping the Lists in a "Lists" submenu independent of the number of lists the user has, but we should only show that submenu when the user has over 2 lists.

So if the user has 2 or fewer, they should be displayed directly in the menu root.

![image](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/57e663d9-218f-4a58-a447-05a86406ea86)

Internal ref: pcdRpT-4tr-p2#comment-7237

Pages: When publishing pages you see the prepublish modal that tells you select catergoris ### Expected behaviour You should not see the prepublish modal and you should not be able to choose categories. ### Actual behaviour You see this. After you publish a page. ### Steps to reproduce the behavior Create a new page. Publish it. ##### Tested on Pixel 3, Android 12, JPAndroid / WPAndroid 23.6-RC-1

row.problem_statement: "(∞)" shouldn't be used for infinite percentages in stats screens ### Expected behavior When the percentage is `∞` for data in stats screens, we should only show the increase. ### Actual behavior When the percentage is `∞` for data in stats screens, it shows `(∞)`. ### Steps to reproduce the behavior 1. Launch the Jetpack app. (WordPress app has the same issue too, but Jetpack has more places showing `(∞)`.) 2. Open Stats from "My Site → HOME → Stats" it "My Site → MENU → Stats". 3. If the Views & Visitors card is not added to the screen, add it by using ⚙️ button at the top end of the screen. 4. Find a case which "Previous Week" had 0 views, and "This week" has more than 0 views. 5. Notice `(∞)` is being shown as the percentage. The problem is also on Total Likes card, Total Comments card, and the bar chart on DAYS, WEEKS, MONTHS, YEARS tabs. ##### Tested on Pixel 4 emulator, Android 11.0, WPAndroid 20.9-rc-2

row.problem_statement:
[Prompts] Regression in Prompts responses list pagination
### Expected behavior
When tapping the `View all responses` and scrolling the response list I should be able to get an infinite scroll with proper pagination until I see all responses. New posts are added to the end of the list when loaded. (it was working on version 23.6 and below)

### Actual behavior
When tapping the `View all responses` and scrolling the response list I see the new responses loading once or twice but then no more posts are loaded. Posts shift places when a load happens. (this behavior started on version 23.7)

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
On any Jetpack app build of version 23.7 or later do the following:
1. Install and log into the Jetpack app
2. Make sure the `dailyprompt-XXXX` tag for the current day IS NOT FOLLOWED
3. Go to the dashboard
4. Tap `View all responses` on the Prompts card
5. Scroll the prompt tag responses list for a while
6. **Verify** pagination is not working properly (posts are shifting around)
7. **Verify** pagination stops loading new posts at some point

##### Tested on Emulator, Android 14, JPAndroid 23.7 and later

row.problem_statement:
Improve wording of no connection message
We use the strings "No network available" (title) and "There is no network available" (message) together in several places when we detect there's no network connection. As mentioned [here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/pull/21275#discussion_r1790413203), this is redundant and unhelpful. We should use better wording, or simply use only the title string and no message.

row.problem_statement:
[BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))
row.hints_text:
Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing

```
"Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')"
```

Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid.

Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that)
The options I see:

### 1. Disable CORS

That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve`

Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol

### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL

Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues.

In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have  CORS issues

By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved.

That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future.

I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method:

https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26

Is something like that possible, @dae?

edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being

row.problem_statement:
Group commands by categories
Currently we have 39 possible commands in the control preferences (if I counted them right), which is a big number. 

Besides searching, to find a command more easily, I suggest grouping them in some categories, like:

- Answer buttons
    - Show answer
    - Answer button 1
    - Answer button 2
    - ...
- Flags
    - red
    - blue
    - orange
    - ...
- Whiteboard
    - ...
- Current card/note
    - suspend
    - delete
    - bury
    - ...
- Navigation
    - Page up
    - Page down
    - Abort
    - ...
- Misc
    - whatever couldn't be put in any other categories
row.hints_text:
Implementation-wise, this probably means that the commands shouldn't be created programatically, so it would be nice to add a test to check if all commands were added to the XML
i think im understanding how commands work so i would like to do it

diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt
index b6d80b1e9598..4cc4cda43a52 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt
@@ -1220,6 +1220,10 @@ open class Reviewer :
                 showDueDateDialog()
                 return true
             }
+            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE -> {
+                toggleAutoAdvance()
+                return true
+            }
             ViewerCommand.USER_ACTION_1 -> {
                 userAction(1)
                 return true
@@ -1498,6 +1502,21 @@ open class Reviewer :
         }
     }
 
+    private fun toggleAutoAdvance() {
+        if (automaticAnswer.isDisabled) {
+            Timber.i("Enabling auto advance")
+            automaticAnswer.enable()
+            if (isDisplayingAnswer) {
+                automaticAnswer.delayedShowQuestion(0)
+            } else {
+                automaticAnswer.delayedShowAnswer(0)
+            }
+        } else {
+            Timber.i("Disabling auto advance")
+            automaticAnswer.disable()
+        }
+    }
+
     override val currentCardId: CardId?
         get() = currentCard!!.id

row.problem_statement:
Add `Toggle auto advance` control/shortcut
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Sometimes during review you need to pause Auto Advance, unlike Anki desktop which I only click on shift+A to pause/resume the feature, on Ankidroid you need to exit the review session and toggle it, which is not practical at all.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A button to switch Auto Advance like the Enable/Disable whiteboard feature during review.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
x
**Additional context**
x

I'd like to work on it

@@ -241,6 +241,8 @@
                 <data android:mimeType="application/octet-stream"/>
                 <data android:mimeType="text/tab-separated-values"/>
                 <data android:mimeType="text/comma-separated-values"/>
+                <data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>
+                <data android:mimeType="text/tsv"/>
             </intent-filter>
 
             <!-- Keep it separate as when sharing image AnkiDroid would be shown with Image Occlusion label -->
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt
index d6164158555d..6f2075c69247 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt
@@ -299,6 +299,14 @@ class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
         private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT = "com.ichi2.anki.COPY_DEBUG_INFO"
         private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA = "clip_data"
 
+        private val textMimeTypes =
+            setOf(
+                "text/tab-separated-values",
+                "text/tsv",
+                "text/comma-separated-values",
+                "text/csv",
+            )
+
         private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
             // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
             // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
@@ -333,8 +341,7 @@ class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
                 val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
                 when {
                     mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
-                    mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
-                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
+                    textMimeTypes.contains(mimeType) -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
                     else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
                 }
             } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {

row.problem_statement:
User report: Can't see or open CSVs
### Checked for duplicates?

- [x] This issue is not a duplicate

### Does it also happen in the desktop version?

- [x] This bug does not occur in the latest version of Anki Desktop

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Exact steps unknown

https://www.reddit.com/r/Anki/comments/1i8b5p3/best_way_to_easily_create_flashcards_on_ankidroid/

User is on Discord and responsive: https://discord.com/channels/368267295601983490/701939078786187315/1332453507789357116

Requested a screenshot of the file in the location, and a screenshot of AnkiDroid not picking it up


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/429cc47f-d727-4121-b7d9-e11de1788a81)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9bd58b28-7363-466c-880a-6145a9a5b508)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e7be5d0c-2a20-4369-8e56-d63d07fbaa76)



### Expected behaviour

The CSV file is visible

### Debug info

```

### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

While testing, I was having huge problems with 'saving' CSVs in Google Drive, but they didn't appear in the folder I saved them in.

I don't trust files on Android at all

### Research

- [x] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue

+
+package com.ichi2.anki.previewer
+
+import androidx.lifecycle.Lifecycle
+import androidx.test.core.app.ActivityScenario
+import androidx.test.espresso.Espresso
+import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
+import com.ichi2.anki.RobolectricTest
+import com.ichi2.anki.browser.PreviewerIdsFile
+import com.ichi2.testutils.createTransientDirectory
+import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
+import org.junit.Test
+import org.junit.runner.RunWith
+
+@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
+class PreviewerFragmentTest : RobolectricTest() {
+
+    @Test
+    fun `previewer - back button`() {
+        val note = addNoteUsingBasicAndReversedModel()
+
+        val intent = PreviewerFragment.getIntent(
+            targetContext,
+            previewerIdsFile = PreviewerIdsFile(createTransientDirectory(), note.cardIds(col)),
+            currentIndex = 0
+        )
+
+        ActivityScenario.launch<PreviewerActivity>(intent).use { scenario ->
+            scenario.moveToState(Lifecycle.State.RESUMED)
+            scenario.onActivity { previewer ->
+                assertThat("Activity is not finishing", !previewer.isFinishing)
+                // this needs to test the keypress, not the dispatcher
+                Espresso.pressBack()
+                assertThat("Activity is finishing after back press", previewer.isFinishing)
+            }
+        }
+    }
+}

row.problem_statement: Improve "discard changes" dialog options If you click Add note and type something and then close the window without saving the changes, the following message pops up. This pop up also appears at other places (for e.g. when leaving the Deck Options page without clicking Save). The issue with this pop up is that "OK" and "Cancel" don't seem to be the correct responses to a message that says "Close and lose current input". What about using the following instead? - Anki Desktop: - Google Photos:

row.problem_statement: [BUG]: Answer comparison not shown when type answer into the card is enabled ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? - Enable `Advanced → Type answer into the card` - Review a card that requires typing the answer. ### Expected behaviour After clicking "Show Answer", the comparision of the typed and actual answers should be shown. It is the case when the above-mentioned Advanced option is disabled. ### Actual behaviour Only the correct answer is shown. ### Debug info ```text 2.17 beta 4 ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? This might be a long-standing bug. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

index 09159098ade3..8e62cebf3a32 100644

@@ -21,6 +21,7 @@ import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
 import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
 import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
 import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
+import com.ichi2.libanki.redo

@@ -42,3 +43,21 @@ suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH
         showSnackbar(message, duration)
     }
 }
+
+suspend fun FragmentActivity.redoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
+    withProgress {
+        val changes = undoableOp {
+            if (redoAvailable()) {
+                redo()
+            } else {
+                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
+            }
+        }
+        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
+            TR.actionsNothingToRedo()
+        } else {
+            TR.undoActionRedone(changes.operation)
+        }
+        showSnackbar(message, duration)
+    }
+}

row.problem_statement:
Add option to Redo Answer Card
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Sometimes, I accidentally click on the Undo button in the reviewer, which undoes the rating of the last card I reviewed.
This happens especially when I am editing a particular card during review because the location of the ✓ button of the edit note window coincides with the location of the undo button.

Because AnkiDroid doesn't have the Redo button, I have to rethink which answer button should be used for rating that card.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
This problem can be solved by adding a Redo option, just like Desktop Anki has.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.
row.hints_text:
Really liked the feature request. I'd try to implement it  
I've been working a bit on implementing this feature and had some questions for other contributers. Would a redo button like this also stay active while the whiteboard is up to redo strokes? I believe that would make the most sense, but I want to see if anyone has any objections. 
So, as of #14171, general redo suport should be practical to implement. The whiteboard is another matter, and may need its own undo/redo buttons.
Im gonna do it

row.problem_statement:
Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.
row.hints_text:
I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt

 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {

row.problem_statement:
[BUG]: display order bug for notes
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Go to "card browser" then "options" and under "toggle cards/notes" select "notes". Go to "change display order" and choose another option.

### Expected behaviour

The display order should stay as set, like when "cards" is selected in "toggle cards/notes."

### Actual behaviour

When you select "notes", it automatically sets the display order to "by created time" oldest to newest. If you choose another option it doesn't change except by pressing "by created time" again to flip the direction.  This only works until you exit the app or add a new note, and it then flips to oldest to newest again.

### Debug info

```text
AnkiDroid Version = 2.16.5 (953b9bd879269910ee962b520da9705336d8bc2d)

Android Version = 13

ProductFlavor = play

Manufacturer = samsung

Model = SM-P610

Hardware = exynos9611

Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; SM-P610 Build/TP1A.220624.014; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/119.0.6045.66 Safari/537.36

ACRA UUID = cc3ec77b-8b96-40ad-99e3-3187c7799494

New schema = false

Scheduler = std2

Crash Reports Enabled = true

DatabaseV2 Enabled = true
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

row.problem_statement: [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6)
row.hints_text: It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix
